user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object part (rear bumper/rear of the car), allowing for an assessment of the alleged dent.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a new dent on the rear bumper area. Image img_1 shows the rear of the car, and while no explicit damage is noted in the observations, the image itself provides sufficient context to evaluate the claim for deformation or dents.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provide visual evidence (img_1) showing a dent on the fender, which is relevant to assessing vehicle body damage claims. The combination of observations allows for an evaluation of surface marks/deformation.
claim_status_justification: The customer specifically claimed a 'scratch' on the front bumper. While img_1 shows a dent on the fender, the overall evidence set (img_1 and img_2) confirms visible damage areas near the front of the car, supporting the general claim of surface/body damage in that area.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show cracks and scratches on the claimed object (windshield), meeting the minimum evidence requirements for a crack/broken part claim.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a crack from a small stone impact, and img_1 explicitly shows multiple cracks across the windshield. This directly supports the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image observation does not clearly show or confirm the alleged damage to the side mirror, failing to meet the minimum evidence requirement for inspecting broken parts.
claim_status_justification: The provided image observation does not contain sufficient visual confirmation of the damaged side mirror to support or contradict the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims 'bumper damage' to the rear bumper after being tapped from behind. However, both provided images (img_1 and img_2) show no visible damage on the body or rear bumper area, contradicting the claim.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims damage to the rear bumper, but the visual observations for both img_1 and img_2 indicate no visible damage on the body or rear bumper area.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims the headlight is cracked, but the only provided image (img_1) shows the car with 'no visible damage' and does not provide a close-up or clear view of the headlight area to confirm a crack.
claim_status_justification: The customer specifically claims the headlight is cracked, but the single provided image (img_1) does not show a visible crack or sufficient detail of the headlight to support this claim.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The minimum evidence requirements for a dent or scratch are met because img_2 clearly shows a visible scratch on the claimed object part (door) and is of good quality.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims there is a dent along the side of the car door. While img_1 is too blurry, img_2 clearly shows a visible scratch on the lower section of the car door, supporting a surface mark claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object (car) and the relevant part (hood), allowing for an assessment of visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a scratch on the hood, and img_1 shows the car's hood open with visible damage to the front end area.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object (laptop screen/lid) and the alleged damage (multiple visible cracks), meeting the minimum evidence requirements for a screen or lid claim.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the display glass has a crack, and img_1 shows multiple visible cracks across the laptop lid surface.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims the hinge area is broken and the screen wobbles due to a drop. However, both provided images (img_1 and img_2) explicitly state that there is no visible damage to the body or general laptop structure, failing to provide visual evidence of the claimed breakage or wobble.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the hinge area is broken and wobbles. The provided image observations (img_1, img_2) both report 'no visible damage' to the body or general laptop structure, contradicting the claim of breakage.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object (laptop) and the relevant part (keyboard/body), displaying visible water damage as described by the customer.
claim_status_justification: The image observation for img_1 explicitly notes 'visible water damage on the surface of a laptop keyboard and body,' directly supporting the customer's claim of spilling water near the laptop and leaving a stain/sticky keys.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provide visual evidence (img_1 shows the lid, img_2 shows the body) that are relevant to the claimed object (laptop) and parts (corner/body). Although the customer claims a dent on a corner, img_2 shows a spot on the body, which is related but not explicitly confirming a 'dent' on a specific corner. However, since the images show the general area of the laptop body and lid, they meet the minimum requirement for reviewability.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a dent on a corner. While img_2 shows a spot on the laptop body, neither image clearly and definitively shows a dent specifically located on a visible corner of the laptop that can be assessed against the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object part (screen) with the claimed issue type (crack), meeting the minimum evidence requirements.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims screen damage, and img_1 provides clear visual evidence of multiple spiderweb cracks across the laptop screen.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims physical damage around the trackpad area. While img_1 shows the general body of the laptop, it is a closed view and does not provide sufficient visual evidence to inspect the specific claimed area (trackpad/base) for physical damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims physical damage around the trackpad area, but the only provided image (img_1) is a closed view of the laptop body and does not show the claimed damaged part for inspection.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The customer claims one corner was crushed, but img_1 shows a cardboard box with no visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the corner was crushed, but the only provided image (img_1) shows no visible damage to the box.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The minimum evidence requirements for 'crushed, torn, or seal damage' are met by img_1, which clearly shows scratches and tears on the package exterior.
claim_status_justification: The customer specifically requested a review for torn packaging. img_1 provides clear visual evidence of scratches and tears on the box, supporting the claim of damaged packaging.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The single provided image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object (package) and the specific damage type (torn packaging/water stain area).
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the package has a wet-looking stain and surface damage. Image img_1 shows visible packaging damage (a tear) on the side of the package, supporting the claim of external damage.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: While img_1 shows an open box and contents, it does not provide clear evidence of missing items or confirm that the claimed object (the product) was absent. The minimum requirement for 'contents or inner item' is not met because there is no visual confirmation of what should be inside or proof of absence.
claim_status_justification: The images show the open package and contents (img_1), but they do not provide sufficient visual evidence to confirm that a specific item is missing. The claim requires proof of absence, which cannot be visually verified from the provided observations.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Only one image is provided (img_1), which shows the general side of the box but does not clearly show the specific 'crushed' damage claimed by the user. The observation notes that the image shows a box with visible nutrition facts printed on the side, but there is no explicit visual confirmation or clear view of crushing damage.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the box was 'badly crushed,' but the single provided image (img_1) only shows a general view of the side with nutrition facts and does not provide sufficient visual evidence to confirm or assess the claimed crushing damage.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show the general condition of the box/package exterior (img_1) and the side of the package with labels (img_2), which is sufficient to review for torn or opened seals as requested.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims the seal area looked torn upon receipt. While the provided images do not explicitly show a tear or open seal, they confirm that the package exterior (img_1) and side (img_2) are visible for review, meeting the minimum evidence requirement to assess the claim.
